title: Move stable Morty persona/role into --append-system-prompt to maximize prompt-cache hits
source: Anthropic docs: Prompt caching — https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-caching ; Claude Code CLI --append-system-prompt flag
description: Each delegation inlines the full static persona/role instructions into the user prompt (safe_prompt at delegate.py:1762-1824). Because the static prefix changes position with every ticket-specific body, automatic prompt caching can't reuse it across the many delegations in a sprint. Moving the invariant persona/role/system content to a stable system prompt via --append-system-prompt lets the CLI's automatic caching reuse the cached prefix, cutting input cost (cache reads are ~10% of base input price) and latency for repeated same-role Morty calls.
proposed_changes: Split prompt construction into a stable system segment (persona, role contract, output protocol, handoff schema — sourced from persona.py) and a volatile user segment (ticket body, team context, effort note). Pass the stable segment via cmd.extend(['--append-system-prompt', system_text]) before appending the user prompt at delegate.py:1824. Keep the segment byte-identical per role so the cache prefix is stable; add a _check_claude_append_system_support() guard mirroring the existing _check_claude_thinking_support() pattern, falling back to inlining if the flag is unsupported.
